explanation: The requirement states that once the controller enters fail-safe mode, it cannot switch to any other mode unless a complete power cycle (turn off and then on) is performed. With the available atomic propositions, 'failSafeMode' indicates entry into fail-safe mode, and 'off' indicates that the system is currently off (de-energized). Thus, as long as the system has not been powered off and then on, being in fail-safe mode should prevent transition to other modes. Since there are no directly available atomic propositions for the system being turned on, we can model it such that if the system enters 'failSafeMode', it must remain in 'failSafeMode' until 'off' is true (the system is turned off). Once 'off' occurs, normal operation (i.e., exiting fail-safe mode) is permitted again. The structured NL property captures this by saying: whenever in fail-safe mode, remain in fail-safe mode until the system is off.
decision1_substring: The controller cannot return from fail-safe mode to any other mode without a power cycle
decision3_substring: cannot return from fail-safe mode to any other mode without a power cycle (turn off and then turn on the machine)
output_structured_natural_language: whenever failSafeMode, until off, satisfy failSafeMode

explanation: The requirement states that once the controller is in fail-safe mode, it cannot return to any other mode (i.e., must remain in fail-safe mode) until a power cycle (the machine is turned off and then on). This is formalized by ensuring that after entering fail-safe mode, the controller continues to stay in fail-safe mode unless the 'off' atomic proposition becomes true (the system is completely de-energized), modelling a restart requirement. The structured NL expresses this by stating: 'after failSafeMode, until off, always satisfy failSafeMode', meaning once in fail-safe mode, stay there until the system is completely powered down (off).

decision3_substring: cannot return from fail-safe mode to any other mode without a power cycle (turn off and then turn on the machine)

explanation: The requirement states that once the controller enters fail-safe mode, it cannot return to any other mode unless the machine is power-cycled. That is, the only way to exit fail-safe mode is by turning the machine off (where 'off' is true) and then on again. Using the provided atomic propositions, we formalize that after entering 'failSafeMode', it is never the case that any other mode becomes active unless the 'off' state has occurred in-between. As there is no atomic proposition directly matching switching into a specific non-fail-safe operational mode, and 'failSafeMode' and 'off' are provided, the structured NL templates are chosen to specify that once 'failSafeMode' is true, it always remains true until 'off' is true (i.e., the ventilation system is turned off). This prevents any return from fail-safe to any other mode without power cycling.

explanation: This property captures the intent of the requirement that the controller, once in fail-safe mode, cannot transition out of it unless there has been a power cycle (the system has turned off and on). This is enforced using atomic propositions: 'failSafeMode' (indicates the controller is in fail-safe mode) and 'off' (indicates system is completely de-energized and off). The Boolean expression 'failSafeMode & !off' depicts the interval in which the system is in fail-safe mode and is not off. During this period, the requirement is that the system must always remain in 'failSafeMode'; i.e., the controller cannot exit fail-safe mode unless the system turns off (power cycle). Thus, using the template 'while bool_exp1, always satisfy bool_exp3', setting bool_exp1 to 'failSafeMode & !off' and bool_exp3 to 'failSafeMode', enforces this restriction until the system is powered down. No additional temporal event or duration is specified, and only these two atomic propositions are needed for the translation.

explanation: The requirement specifies that once the controller enters fail-safe mode, it must remain in that mode and cannot transition to any other mode until the machine is power cycled (turned off and on). Given the available atomic propositions, 'failSafeMode' (indicating the system is in fail-safe mode) and 'off' (system is powered off) are most relevant. We need to express that as long as 'failSafeMode' holds, the only way to exit this state is first to become 'off' (power cycle). Thus, structured NL will capture that while in fail-safe mode, the controller cannot stop being in fail-safe mode without the system first having turned off (i.e., observing 'off' at some point before exiting 'failSafeMode' is required). This is captured as 'while failSafeMode, always satisfy (failSafeMode | off)'. This expresses that no state outside of fail-safe mode can occur except after 'off'.

explanation: The requirement states that once the controller enters fail-safe mode, it cannot return to any other mode until a power cycle (turn off and then turn on the machine) occurs. In terms of atomic propositions, 'failSafeMode' represents being in fail-safe mode and 'off' represents the system being completely de-energized and off. Therefore, while the system is in fail-safe mode, no transitions away from failSafeMode (i.e., not failSafeMode) are allowed unless the system state becomes 'off' (representing the machine is turned off as part of a power cycle). This is captured by saying: while failSafeMode, always satisfy (failSafeMode | off). This means that as long as failSafeMode is true, at every time step, either failSafeMode continues to hold or the system switches off (initiating a power cycle); transitions to other (non-failsafe) modes are thus blocked without a power cycle.

explanation: The requirement states that once the system enters fail-safe mode, it cannot return to any other mode (i.e., cannot exit fail-safe mode) except by being power cycled (turned off and then on again). To capture this, we need to formally state that after failSafeMode becomes true, it must remain true (i.e., the system remains in fail-safe mode) until the system is completely powered off (off becomes true). The structured NL property chosen is 'until bool_exp4, satisfy bool_exp3' with bool_exp3 = failSafeMode and bool_exp4 = off. This captures the intent that the controller cannot exit fail-safe mode (failSafeMode remains true) unless it is first turned off (off becomes true, i.e., power cycle).
